items.title: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
items.summary: "I don't think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation," one expert told TechCrunch.

items.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
items.summary: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.

items.title: After shocking quarter, IBM insists that AI isn’t killing the mainframe
items.summary: After IBM's stock crashed last week on warnings of poor mainframe sales, the CEO explained that AI wrecked corporate hardware budget, temporarily.

items.title: Google justifies its massive AI spending with a booming cloud business
items.summary: Google's cloud business is thriving, as companies adopting its AI and AI infrastructure services help the tech giant to report record profits.

items.title: Treasury threatens sanctions after White House claims Moonshot distilled Anthropic’s Fable
items.summary: The episode has also intensified a broader debate in Washington over the influx of Chinese open models.

items.title: Tesla spending skyrockets as Cybercab, Semi, Megapack production timeline slips
items.summary: Tesla's 26% boost in revenue wasn't enough to offset rising operating expenses and capital expenditures as it pushes to launch a new generation of products.

items.title: SoundCloud acquires decentralized music platform Nina Protocol months after its shutdown
items.summary: SoundCloud has acquired decentralized music platform Nina Protocol, months after the startup announced it would shut down. The deal brings Nina’s artists, editorial archive, and music discovery tools...

items.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face

items.title: Travis Kalanick’s robotics company raises $1.7B, led by a16z
items.summary: Uber is also investing in Travis Kalanick's company Atoms, which has made gauzy claims about using industrial AI to modernize the world.

items.title: Science Corporation’s vision-restoring chip wins EU approval
items.summary: "The thing that the space needs is a company making $100 million a year of revenue," Science Corp. CEO Max Hodak said.

items.title: Yope raises $12.3M to build a private social network without algorithms or ads

items.title: Menlo Ventures’ Matt Murphy explains why Anthropic is winning (and it’s not the model)

items.title: Monday.com lays off hundreds to focus on AI
items.summary: The company said it is reducing its headcount by 20%, or about 630 staff, to "support a leaner, more focused operating model" as it focuses on its AI Work Platform.

items.title: Google is making it easier to switch from iPhone to Android
items.summary: The new feature lets users wirelessly transfer more data types from an iPhone without needing to download a separate app.

items.title: Arcee, a US open source AI lab, says Chinese models are not inherently dangerous
items.summary: As Chinese AI models grow in capability and popularity among U.S. companies, the arguing over what should be done about them has reached a fever pitch.

items.title: Substack’s new tool tells you who’s been writing their newsletters with AI
items.summary: Substack is giving readers a way to estimate how much of a newsletter was written by AI, signaling a broader shift toward transparency around AI-assisted content.

items.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
items.summary: OpenAI will spend the equivalent of Sweden's GDP on infrastructure through 2030.

items.title: Cascade raises $3.5M to help construction firms find and win projects
